title: Values, Types, Coercion, and Equality · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Normalize prices, quantities, identifiers, optional fields, and validity signals from a mixed service-data feed. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a value-normalization and validation pipeline after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish undefined, null, Boolean, Number, BigInt, String, Symbol, and object values by behavior and use.
Use typeof, Array.isArray, instanceof, and explicit predicates while accounting for their boundaries.
Predict implicit coercion and prefer explicit conversion at external data boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Primitive Types evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Primitive Types assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Normalize service inputs [" 2 ", 2, null, {value:2}] without implicit-coercion surprises. Record typeof, equality, parsed value, and the exact branch that rejects the object case.

content.text: Order work so Primitive Types has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
